title: Carramboa × tachirensis
intro: Carramboa × tachirensis, also known as the Tachiren Carramboa, is a hybrid plant species native to the Andean region of South America, belonging to the family Asteraceae. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which provide relief from pain and promote wound healing. The bioactive constituents of Carramboa × tachirensis include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Carramboa × tachirensis is commonly prepared as a tea or infusion, and also used to make topical ointments and creams for the treatment of skin conditions.
